summary: Build an Instagram-like app where users upload images; improve overall app performance by adding browser caching, optimizing image loading, and implementing optimistic loading (optimistic UI updates) across the app to reduce perceived latency.
architectural_notes: User-uploaded images should be served with effective browser caching (cache-control/etag) and optimized delivery (compression/resizing, lazy loading) to reduce load times across the app.
Consider optimistic UI/optimistic loading patterns across the app: update UI immediately, run server request in background, and rollback/reconcile on failure.
known_issues: Image load times can be slow when navigating through the app; needs caching/performance optimization.

existing_features.title: Add client-side image optimization on upload to reduce file size and improve perceived load time across the app (e.g., downscale large images before upload and compress appropriately) while keeping acceptable visual quality.

Improve overall app navigation speed by reducing unnecessary refetching and proactively fetching key data needed for the next view (especially the feed after login and user posts on profile navigation).

Implement optimistic UI updates (optimistic loading) consistently across the app so key actions feel instant while the backend request completes in the background. This must cover at minimum: post like/unlike, comment add, comment like/unlike, follow/unfollow, post create/delete, and profile save.

Update React Query mutation flows to avoid unnecessary broad refetching after mutations by using optimistic cache updates (queryClient.setQueryData) and targeted invalidation only when required, while still guaranteeing eventual consistency with the backend.

Implement consistent optimistic UI updates (optimistic loading) for all key mutations by moving optimistic behavior into React Query mutation hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` using `onMutate` (optimistic cache update), `onError` (rollback), and minimal targeted invalidation/refetch only when required for eventual consistency.

Update UI components to rely on the unified optimistic React Query cache state rather than per-component local optimistic state where applicable, ensuring identical optimistic behavior in all entry points (e.g., liking from feed card vs. post detail modal; liking comments from comments modal vs. post detail modal).

Make post create/delete feel instant by applying optimistic cache updates to the feed (`['feed']`) and user posts (`['userPosts', principal]`) lists so navigation back to feed/profile does not require a full refetch to reflect the change.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add browser caching and general performance optimizations (especially for user-uploaded images) to speed up overall app loading.
Enable effective browser caching for all user-uploaded images served from the canister so that revisiting the feed/profile reuses cached images instead of re-downloading them.
Implement optimistic loading/optimistic UI updates across the app (e.g., likes, comments, posting, navigation) so key actions and views feel instant while server requests complete in the background.

projectName: Add browser caching and performance optimizations for user-uploaded images
